text: Air pollution exposure is identified as one of the major risk factors for a vast array of fatal diseases and thus accurate air quality forecasting is needed to facilitate timely preventive actions. Therefore, the provision of reliable air quality forecasting systems is crucial for the protection of human health. In recent years, Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) techniques have been widely adopted for air quality prediction. This paper delivers a thorough systematic review of the major works published on air quality prediction using ML and DL from 2011 to 2021. Through an in-depth search of the major scientific literature databases, 155 research papers were identified and critically assessed. The extracted papers are systematically classified, examined and presented in relation to the location, the pollutants to be predicted, input features, performance evaluation criteria and ML techniques employed.
explanation: The text resembles a typical abstract for a systematic review, with plausible details (2011–2021, 155 papers). Its highly generic phrasing leaves some uncertainty about AI generation but could easily be human-written.
